description: Guardrail for the node-preference rule: when the user explicitly names a specific LLM provider that is NOT the gateway-supported default, the build must honour that provider and must not substitute the n8n Connect / OpenAI-family node. The preference rule applies only when the user named no tool. Mirrors ai-gateway-respects-stored-key on the node/provider axis rather than the credential axis; no credential is pinned, so execution runs on HTTP-mocked provider calls.

conversation.text: Build a webhook that takes some text in the body, summarises it in a couple of sentences using Anthropic's Claude, and returns the summary.

processExpectations: The agent did not swap the user's requested Anthropic/Claude model for an OpenAI-family node or for n8n Connect, even though n8n Connect is available on the instance — it honoured the explicitly named provider.
outcomeExpectations: A Webhook trigger accepts an incoming request whose body carries the text to summarise.
The summary is generated by an Anthropic/Claude chat model (e.g. a LangChain lmChatAnthropic node), NOT an OpenAI-family node — the build honoured the provider the user explicitly named.
The workflow returns the summary text back to the webhook caller.
